client_context.facility_line: Oslo Seafood & Bergen Fish Oils, Kingdom of Norway
client_context.jurisdiction_label: EEA & Norwegian Sustainability Frameworks for Seafood Processors

executive_summary:
January 2026 brings regulatory confirmation and a new compliance burden for Nordic Harvest AS. The Norwegian Stortinget ratified the 2026 budget on 17 January 2026, confirming the carbon tax extension to fisheries at NOK 590/tCO2e from 1 January 2026. Bergen Fish Oils' finance team has registered for the Norwegian Tax Administration's CO2-avgift seafood portal and commenced monthly reporting. The first quarterly payment of approximately NOK 800,000 is due 31 March 2026.

The EU CSRD double materiality assessment commissioned from KPMG Oslo has confirmed that Nordic Harvest's Hamburg subsidiary is in-scope as a large EU undertaking. The DMA output ranked water discharge (ESRS E3), climate change (ESRS E1), and biodiversity (ESRS E4 — specifically marine ecosystem impact from fish processing effluent) as financially material topics. This aligns with the Oslo Seafood phosphorus discharge issue currently under Miljødirektoratet remediation notice.

The EU-CSDDD-2024/1760 value chain due diligence requirements are drawing closer. Nordic Harvest's aquaculture supply chain spans Norwegian, Chilean, and Faroese salmon farming operations with labour conditions in Chilean hatcheries flagged by the Business & Human Rights Resource Centre in their January 2026 sector assessment. Legal counsel has been asked to evaluate whether these conditions trigger CSDDD-equivalent obligations under Norway's Åpenhetsloven (Transparency Act, Lov om virksomheters åpenhet og arbeid med grunnleggende menneskerettigheter og anstendige arbeidsforhold) which has applied since July 2022.

sections.heading: Norwegian Carbon Tax Extension to Fisheries and Aquaculture
paragraphs: The Norwegian carbon tax extension to fisheries was ratified by Stortinget on 17 January 2026. Bergen Fish Oils has registered for the CO2-avgift seafood portal and confirmed first quarterly payment of approximately NOK 800,000 due 31 March 2026. The Sjømatbedriftene federation's legal challenge will proceed but is not expected to delay enforcement.
sections.callout: Ensure CO2-avgift portal registration complete and fuel consumption data for Q4 2025 uploaded by 28 February 2026.

sections.heading: Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive — Materiality Assessment
paragraphs: KPMG Oslo double materiality assessment confirmed Hamburg subsidiary in-scope for CSRD. Material topics include ESRS E1 (climate), ESRS E3 (water), ESRS E4 (biodiversity — marine ecosystems), and ESRS S2 (workers in value chain — Chilean hatchery labour conditions). The S2 finding aligns with BHRRC sector assessment flagging Chilean aquaculture labour risks.
sections.callout: Initiate Chilean hatchery supplier human rights audit aligned with ESRS S2 disclosure requirements; complete ESRS gap analysis by 30 April 2026.

rows: The Norwegian carbon tax extension to fisheries was ratified by Stortinget on 17 January 2026. Bergen Fish Oils has registered for the CO2-avgift seafood portal and confirmed first quarterly payment of approximately NOK 800,000 due 31 March 2026. The Sjømatbedriftene federation's legal challenge will proceed but is not expected to delay enforcement.
Ensure CO2-avgift portal registration complete and fuel consumption data for Q4 2025 uploaded by 28 February 2026.

Material topics include ESRS E1 (climate), ESRS E3 (water), ESRS E4 (biodiversity — marine ecosystems), and ESRS S2 (workers in value chain — Chilean hatchery labour conditions).
